data.title: Cruise line stocks stage a mini-comeback after a recent sell-off due to tariff and recession concerns
data.description:
The cruise line sector broke higher on Tuesday after Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said about 70 countries have approached the U.S. for tariff negotiations. "If they come to the table with solid proposals, I think we can end up with some good deals," Bessent told CNBC.

The relief rally is tied to investor hopes that a recession in the U.S. that impacts spending on travel can be avoided through a quick resolution of the tariff standoff.

There was also a notable cruise line strategy update for investors to digest this week. Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) announced on Monday that the company struck long-term charter agreements for four vessels across its three brands. The deals include purchase options.

Two Norwegian Cruise Line ships, Norwegian Sky and Norwegian Sun, will be chartered to Cordelia Cruises, a premium operator in India, starting in 2026 and 2027. Additionally, Regent Seven Seas Cruises’ Seven Seas Navigator and Oceania Cruises’ Insignia will be chartered to Crescent Seas, a residential cruise line, beginning in the same years.

The cruise line company emphasized that the agreements align with its fleet optimization strategy by repurposing vessels for markets beyond its core operations.

Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NLCH) was up 5.1% at 11:22 a.m. while Royal Caribbean (NYSE:RCL) gained 4.6% and Carnival (NYSE:CCL) was up 6.5%. Viking Holdings (NYSE:VIK) led the sector, with a rally of 7.3%.

data.title: Viking Therapeutics draws Neutral rating at Goldman Sachs on competitive headwinds
data.description:
Goldman Sachs launched its coverage of Viking Therapeutics (NASDAQ:VKTX) with a Neutral recommendation and a $30 per share target on Tuesday, arguing that competitive headwinds in the weight loss drug market can overshadow the potential of its lead obesity drug, VK2735.

Citing the Phase 2 data for the dual GLP-1/GIP agonist, analyst Richard Law noted that VK2735 could be a potent threat to injectables and next-gen weight loss therapies in the obesity drug market, which is, however, characterized by intense rivalry.

However, “our extensive analyses of the commercial landscape reveal a hypercompetitive obesity market with high barriers to entry,” Law wrote, pointing to concerns such as ongoing price erosion and tight payer restrictions.

Additionally, unlike the established weight loss therapies such as semaglutide from Novo Nordisk (NVO) and tirzepatide from Eli Lilly (LLY), VK2735 has yet to show its potential against other chronic diseases such as diabetes.

“With the imminent entry of many more next-gen therapies across a variety of mechanisms (Triple G, amylin, etc.), we are less optimistic on VKTX’s ability to compete as a small and less resourceful company,” he added.

data.title: Chevron to survey Mediterranean for pipeline linking Aphrodite gas field to Egypt, Cyprus says
data.description:
Chevron (NYSE:CVX) will begin a survey of the east Mediterranean seabed early this summer to prepare for a pipeline connecting a natural gas deposit in Cyprus waters with processing facilities in Egypt, Cypriot officials said Monday, according to the Associated Press.

The pipeline will link Cyprus' Aphrodite field, which is estimated to contain as much as 4.5T cf of natural gas, with an undisclosed facility on Egypt's coastline.

Cypriot President Nikos Christodoulides was in Houston as part of a weeklong trip to the U.S. to solicit investment in his country, and reportedly met with Chevron (NYSE:CVX) CEO Mike Wirth.

Christodoulides told Wirth that Aphrodite's development is a priority for the Cyprus government, "as it highlights the importance of our country as a new source of supply for the needs of neighboring countries but also the European Union," a government statement said.

Chevron (CVX) and partners Shell and Israeli NewMed Energy are in charge of developing Aphrodite, which was discovered in 2011.

In February, the three countries signed an agreement with Egypt outlining the framework under which Aphrodite will be developed.

data.title: Delta Air Lines Q1 earnings: Eyes on outlook amid softening domestic demand
data.description:
Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) will report its first-quarter earnings on April 9, Wednesday, and investors will focus on its outlook amid worries of softening domestic demand and weakening consumer sentiment.

The consensus EPS Estimate is $0.39 (-13.3% Y/Y) and the consensus Revenue Estimate is $13.46B (-2.1% Y/Y)

The airline sector could face a turbulent time ahead due to increasing economic and geopolitical uncertainty and fears of a rise in inflation due to President Trump's latest tariffs. Goldman Sachs made a “material” cut to the level of demand for air travel in 2025 and lowered its outlook for the U.S. airline industry.

Earlier, Jefferies had also turned cautious on the airline sector and warned that consumer sentiment continued to disappoint. The firm had downgraded Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) to Hold.

"Short-term sentiment on reduced high-end travel is driving the price down, but Delta's earnings growth potential remains strong," Seeking Alpha analyst Noah's Arc Capital Management said.

The combination of cheaper oil, resilient travel demand, and strong profit growth puts Delta in a great position to rebound from this drop-off if it is anything from a temporary blip to a medium term slowdown. I really believe this is a compelling entry point for long-term investors, Noah's Arc Capital Management added.

In March, Delta Air Lines (DAL) slashed its guidance, hurt by the recent reduction in consumer and corporate confidence caused by increased macro uncertainty, driving softness in domestic demand. On the profit line, Delta (DAL) sees Q1 EPS of $0.30 to $0.50 vs. a prior outlook of $0.70 to $1.00.

Delta (DAL) expects to deliver Q1 revenue growth for the March quarter of 3% to 4% year-over-year, vs. a prior outlook of +7% to +9%. 

In the last quarter, the airline firm beat both its top and bottom line estimates. 

"I believe that Delta still has strong fundamentals relative to its peers, but consumer weakness and economic risks warrant caution," Seeking Alpha analyst DM Martins Research said.

Expect more volatility ahead, as the bottom of the well for an airline stock during a recession could surpass a 65% drop from prior highs, DM Martins Research added.

Over the last 2 years, DAL has beaten EPS estimates 63% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 75% of the time.

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 0 upward revisions and 10 downward. Revenue estimates have seen 1 upward revision and 2 downward.

Shares of the company were down 37.2% since the start of the year.

data.title: XRP could surge over tenfold before Trump's term ends, StanChart predicts
data.description:
Standard Chartered predicted Tuesday that the XRP (XRP-USD) cryptocurrency could skyrocket more than tenfold to $12.50 per coin before President Donald Trump's term expires on Jan. 20, 2029.

The token was already up sixfold since crypto-friendly Trump won the November election, amid speculation that the Securities and Exchange Commission would drop its lawsuit against XRP issuer Ripple Labs, as well as a race among asset managers to bring XRP exchange-traded funds to market.

Still, XRP retreated ~18% this year as heightened uncertainty over federal government policies -- chiefly tariffs -- overshadows optimism about pro-crypto measures enacted under the Trump administration.

In any case, StanChart's Geoff Kendrick believes XRP's (XRP-USD) gains since late last year are sustainable, pointing to positive SEC leadership shifts and XRP's prime role in the booming cross-border payments space.

"XRPL is similar to the main use case for stablecoins such as Tether (USDT-USD): blockchain-enabled financial transactions that have traditionally been done through traditional financial (TradFi) institutions," he wrote in a note to clients.

"This stablecoin use has grown 50% annually over the past two years, and we expect stablecoin transactions to increase 10x over the next four years," he added. 

By the end of 2025, Kendrick sees XRP (XRP-USD) reaching $5.50 per coin, implying a ~190% surge from current prices. From there, he expects the token will hit $8 by the end of 2026, $10.40 by end-2027, and $12.50 by end-2028.

Stablecoins are a type of crypto designed to hold a steady value by being pegged to a stable asset, such as the U.S. dollar. 

Also on Tuesday, Ripple said it will buy multi-asset prime brokerage firm Hidden Road for $1.25B, in a deal that's expected to bolster Ripple's efforts on the stablecoin front.

XRP (XRP-USD) was little changed at the time of writing, changing hands at around $1.90 per coin.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Apple risk-reward 'skews positive' when multiple drops below 25: BofA
data.description:
The risk-reward on Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) skews positively three to six months after its earnings multiple has dropped below 25, Bank of America said.

“In recent weeks [Apple] stock has moved significantly to the downside (down 25% YTD and 23.5% since last earnings relative to SPX decline of 13.7% and 16.6% respectively),” analyst Wamsi Mohan wrote in a note to clients, referencing geopolitical uncertainties around tariffs and delays to a more personalized Siri. “In our view, the pullback represents a particularly buying opportunity for investors to own a high-quality name.” Mohan reiterated his Buy rating and $250 price target on the stock.

In the three months after Apple's P/E multiple has fallen below, the share price returned 7%, 8%, 14%, and 17% over the three, six, nine and 12 months after, respectively, Mohan added. Downside in the following three months ranged from a 5% to 11% decline in April and August 2022, but upside ranged from a 11% to 26% increase. 

“Given the geopolitical upheaval with tariffs and risk of potential escalation with China, there is a chance that the risk to estimates could be more severe than reflected in consensus,” Mohan added. However, he explained Apple has a number of options to mitigate downside risk to estimates, including getting more iPhones from India; raising pricing on products and services; pressuring its supply chain for better pricing; ad introducing new products at higher price points and changing the cadence of releases.

Apple shares rose 3.4% in early trading on Tuesday.

data.title: Brera receives FIGC approval for acquisition of Juve Stabia stake
data.description:
• Brera Holdings (NASDAQ:BREA) Tuesday said that the Italian Football Federation has officially completed its review and approved the company’s acquisition of a stake in Juve Stabia, a professional football club currently competing in Italy’s Serie B.
  • Following a comprehensive regulatory process conducted by the FIGC’s Commissione Acquisizioni Partecipazioni Societarie, the commission confirmed that Brera Holdings satisfies the standards of financial soundness and reputational integrity required by FIGC’s regulations.
  • Brera Holdings (NASDAQ:BREA) shares up 7.7% to $0.70.

data.title: Santander evaluating options for $8B Polish stake - Bloomberg
data.description:
Banco Satander (NYSE:SAN) is considering options for its majority stake in its Polish business, including a possible sale. Santander ADRs rose 6.1%. 

The Spanish bank is using an adviser to evaluate its 62% stake in Santander Bank Polska SA and is reaching out to potential investors to see about their interest, according to a Bloomberg report on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Santander's stake in the unit is valued at about $8 billion.

Deliberations are preliminary and Santander (NYSE:SAN) may decide to retain the Polish business or only pursue a partial sale of its stake, according to the report.

Santander declined to comment to Bloomberg.

data.title: Outlook Therapeutics gets FDA review acceptance for wet AMD treatment
data.description:
• Outlook Therapeutics (NASDAQ:OTLK) said on Tuesday that the U.S. FDA has accepted to review ONS-5010, an ophthalmic formulation of bevacizumab, for the treatment of wet age-related macular degeneration.
  • Stock is up 9% in morning trade.
  • The agency has acknowledged receipt of the resubmission of the Biologics License Application for ONS-5010, after the company reported in January that ONS-5010 demonstrated non-inferiority to ranibizumab, sold by Roche's (OTCQX:RHHBY) Genentech unit, at week 12 in a study. 
  • FDA set a Prescription Drug User Fee Act goal date of August 27, 2025.
  • The company  said that ONS-5010, if approved, will be branded as Lytenava in the United States for the treatment of wet AMD and is expected to receive 12 years of regulatory exclusivity.
  • ONS-5010 has already received market authorization in the European Union and United Kingdom.

data.title: Eni to spend €24B in North Africa over next four years, CEO says
data.description:
Eni (NYSE:E) - already a major foreign investor in North Africa's energy sector - expects to spend ~€24B ($26.2B) in Algeria, Libya and Egypt over the next four years to help boost energy production, CEO Claudio Descalzi said Tuesday, according to Reuters.

The CEO said the three countries can play an important role as hydrocarbon suppliers for Europe, but need outside investment to expand their energy production and meet rising domestic demand.

"Internal demand in these countries, because of demographic growth, is increasing at about 7%-8% every year... this means they need gas, they need investment," Descalzi told an energy conference in Ravenna.

The investments would coincide with the Italian government's efforts to relaunch its economic and political ties with Africa as part of its so-called Mattei Plan.

Eni (NYSE:E) will continue to produce gas in Venezuela while engaging with the Trump administration over a waiver to export oil from the country, Descalzi also said.

Eni (E) has been producing gas in Venezuela for the domestic market since 2022, but it is paid in Venezuelan oil, which it exports; the U.S. told Eni last week that it would no longer be able to receive payments for the oil as part of efforts to place secondary sanctions on the country.
